content.problemStatement:
Johny the Farmer has decided to plant potatoes. He quickly realized that he must build a net fence to keep animals from eating his precious plantation. But he only has 3 sticks which can act as vertical poles supporting fence; on the other hand, his supply of net is unlimited. Moreover, since the ground is extremely rocky, the poles can only be positioned in some special places on the field. So, he has decided that the fence must be triangular in shape, with vertices in the distinguished points. Quite naturally, he would also like to maximize the area of the field inside fence -- please help him achieve this goal!

TaskWrite a program which:

reads from the input the coordinates of the points which can hold the poles,
calculates the maximal possible area of the potato plantation.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with an integer n, representing the number of points where poles can be placed. The next line contains n pairs of integers separated by spaces, xi and yi, which are the coordinates for the i-th point.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the maximum possible area of the field inside the fence, multiplied by 2.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The maximum area is obtained by choosing the points (0, 0), (1, 0), and (0, 1). The area of the triangle formed by these points is 1/2, so the output is 2 * 1/2 = 1.
The maximum area is obtained by choosing the points (0, 0), (0, 1), and (1, 1). The area of the triangle formed by these points is 1/2, so the output is 2 * 1/2 = 1.
The maximum area is obtained by choosing the points (0, 0), (1, 0), and (0, 1). The area of the triangle formed by these points is 1/2, so the output is 2 * 1/2 = 1.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by calculating the area of all possible triangles formed by the given points and finding the maximum area. The area of a triangle with vertices (x1, y1), (x2, y2), and (x3, y3) can be calculated using the formula: area = |(x1*(y2-y3) + x2*(y3-y1) + x3*(y1-y2)) / 2|.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * n^3) per test case, O(T * n^3) total.

code.Python3:
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

for _ in range(int(input())):
    n = int(input())
    points = list(map(int, input().split()))
    x = points[::2]
    y = points[1::2]
    maxArea = 0
    for i in range(n):
        for j in range(i + 1, n):
            for k in range(j + 1, n):
                area = abs((x[i]*(y[j]-y[k]) + x[j]*(y[k]-y[i]) + x[k]*(y[i]-y[j])) / 2)
                maxArea = max(maxArea, area)
    print(maxArea * 2)
